segments.caption: The video segment captures a high-intensity first-person shooter gameplay from the game Valorant, featuring a player controlling the character Phoenix. The action begins in a dimly lit corridor at A Main, where the player, equipped with a Vandal rifle adorned with a vibrant blue and pink dragon skin, engages enemies. The player swiftly eliminates multiple opponents using precise gunfire and activates their signature ability, 'Run It Back,' which is visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. The environment shifts between indoor corridors and open outdoor areas with Mediterranean-style architecture, including a gelato shop and colorful walls. The player achieves an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies in one round, followed by a 'CLUTCH' in a subsequent round where they survive with low health and defeat the remaining opponents. The HUD displays Japanese text in later frames, indicating a possible language switch or regional server. The player’s health fluctuates dramatically during combat, and the mini-map consistently shows strategic positioning. The final moments show the player securing a win with a 'WON' notification and a 'CLUTCH' banner, emphasizing their skillful performance under pressure.

discovered_new_entities.Phoenix: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a blue and pink dragon skin, and later a gold and black skin. Uses 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects.
discovered_new_entities.Vandal Rifle: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes from a blue and pink dragon design to a gold and black design. Used for eliminating enemies with high precision, and visible during both indoor and outdoor combat sequences.
discovered_new_entities.Mini-map: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (A Main, B Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation.
discovered_new_entities.Gelato Shop: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.
discovered_new_entities.Japanese HUD Text: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. Appears in later frames, replacing English text.
discovered_new_entities.Run It Back Ability: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. Activated multiple times to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD.

segments.caption: The video begins with a black screen displaying the text 'I want to give a big shoutout' before transitioning into gameplay footage from a first-person shooter game, likely Valorant, featuring vibrant, colorful environments with Mediterranean-style architecture and lush greenery. The player, identified as Phoenix, is seen wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic gold and black skin, engaging in combat in an outdoor area near a Gelato shop. The Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks enemy movements and site locations such as 'A Main' and 'B Site'. As the player moves through the map, the HUD displays Japanese text, including 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language setting change. Phoenix uses the 'Run It Back' ability, shown by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a near-death situation. The scene shifts to a post-match analysis screen with detailed statistics, including Combat Score, Headshot %, and Damage Per Round, overlaid with promotional text for 'Blitz.gg', a performance-tracking tool. The video then cuts to a character selection screen showing Phoenix as a Duelist with a description highlighting his aggressive playstyle. The final frames return to gameplay, with Phoenix navigating a purple-lit indoor area, using the Vandal rifle to eliminate enemies, while the Mini-map continues to guide tactical decisions. Throughout, the video blends gameplay with promotional content for Blitz.gg, emphasizing its analytics and support for various games like Valorant, League of Legends, and Overwatch, with on-screen text in English and Japanese.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a blurred, colorful scene featuring a meme-style frog character with a thinking expression and the text 'and super easy to use', suggesting a promotional or humorous overlay. The scene quickly transitions into first-person gameplay from the game Valorant, where the player, identified as Phoenix, is navigating a futuristic indoor environment with teal and white walls. Phoenix, equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a gold and black skin, moves through corridors and engages enemies with precise gunfire. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently displays team positions and site locations such as 'A Site' and 'B Link'. Phoenix uses his 'Run It Back' ability, indicated by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a near-death situation after being shot. The HUD displays Japanese text, including 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), reflecting a regional server setting. The player then transitions to an outdoor snowy area with blue shipping containers marked 'KNG', engaging enemies in a firefight. After a brief respawn, Phoenix re-enters combat in a Mediterranean-style outdoor area with a Gelato shop visible, continuing to eliminate opponents with the Vandal. The sequence ends with Phoenix using 'Run It Back' again after being hit, showcasing the ability's visual effects and cooldown bar. The environment shifts between indoor corridors, snowy outdoor zones, and sunlit urban areas, with dynamic lighting and particle effects from gunfire and abilities. The gameplay is fast-paced, with frequent enemy encounters and tactical use of abilities, all tracked via the Mini-map and HUD elements.

segments.caption: The video segment follows Phoenix, a Duelist with fiery orange hands and a white jacket bearing an orange flame emblem, as he engages in intense combat across multiple rounds in a vibrant, Mediterranean-style map. Starting at B Site, Phoenix uses his Vandal rifle with a gold and black skin to eliminate an enemy, then activates his 'Run It Back' ability—visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect—to recover from a near-death situation. The environment shifts between outdoor areas with bright blue skies and sunlit architecture to indoor corridors with warm, orange-hued lighting. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently tracks enemy positions and site locations like 'B Main' and 'A Site'. Japanese HUD text appears, including 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language setting change. Phoenix transitions to a Gelato Shop area, where he engages enemies with precise gunfire, and later moves to A Site, using his Vandal to eliminate multiple opponents while navigating tight corridors. The 'Run It Back' ability is used again to survive a critical moment, showcasing Phoenix’s aggressive playstyle. Throughout, the environment features colorful buildings, striped awnings, and dynamic lighting effects, with enemy movements tracked via the Mini-map and broadcasted via on-screen notifications. The segment ends with Phoenix securing kills and maintaining control over key sites, demonstrating tactical precision and ability usage.

segments.caption: The video segment follows Phoenix, a Duelist with fiery orange hands and a white jacket bearing an orange flame emblem, as he engages in intense combat across multiple sites in a fast-paced tactical shooter. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic gold and black skin, Phoenix moves swiftly through various environments—from indoor corridors with blue and beige walls to snowy outdoor areas with industrial structures and a vibrant Gelato Shop. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently tracks enemy positions and site locations (A Main, B Site, C Long), guiding his tactical movements. Phoenix uses his signature ability, Run It Back, multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, to recover from near-death situations, often reappearing mid-combat with full health. Japanese HUD text such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable) appears, indicating a regional server setting. He eliminates enemies with precision using the Vandal, often in close quarters or from elevated positions, and plants the spike at B Site under pressure. The environment shifts from warm-toned interiors to bright, snowy exteriors, with dynamic lighting and particle effects from abilities and gunfire. The sequence culminates in Phoenix surviving a critical encounter, reviving after being downed, and continuing to push forward with renewed aggression, showcasing his aggressive playstyle and reliance on his time-reversal ability.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with Phoenix, identifiable by his fiery orange hands and white jacket with an orange flame emblem, navigating a sunlit outdoor area of a Mediterranean-style map, moving through narrow alleys near the Gelato Shop. He wields a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink skin, engaging enemies with precise gunfire, eliminating multiple opponents in quick succession. The Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks his position near C Long and C Site, showing enemy movements and site control. As the round progresses, Phoenix activates his 'Run It Back' ability, indicated by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, allowing him to recover from a near-death situation and secure an 'ACE' by eliminating all remaining enemies. The scene transitions to a futuristic indoor environment, likely A Site, where Phoenix switches to a Vandal with a gold and black skin, planting the Spike with a Japanese HUD text 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted). He then engages in close-quarters combat, using his abilities and weapon to eliminate enemies while navigating tight corridors. The environment shifts from bright outdoor lighting to a sterile, high-tech indoor setting with glowing panels and metallic surfaces. Japanese text continues to appear on the HUD, including 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. The sequence ends with Phoenix securing the site, showcasing his aggressive playstyle and tactical use of abilities.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with Phoenix, identifiable by his fiery orange hands and white jacket with an orange flame emblem, navigating a futuristic indoor corridor while wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic gold and black skin. He moves swiftly through the map, indicated by the Mini-map in the top-left corner, which shows his progression toward A Site. As he approaches, the environment shifts to an outdoor Mediterranean-style area with warm lighting and architectural details, including the Gelato Shop with its striped awning and ice cream display. Phoenix plants the Spike, triggering the Japanese HUD text 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted), and immediately engages in combat. He uses his 'Run It Back' ability, shown by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a near-death situation. The Vandal rifle’s skin transitions to a vibrant blue and pink design as he eliminates multiple enemies in a rapid sequence, securing an 'ACE' with precise headshots. The Mini-map continues to track enemy movements, and the Japanese HUD text updates to 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable) after he is downed. The scene culminates in a dramatic close-range engagement where Phoenix, now with a melee weapon, finishes off the last enemy, highlighted by the 'ACE' notification and a final burst of visual effects. The environment remains consistent with the map’s blend of modern and traditional design, and the lighting shifts dynamically with combat effects, including purple and orange glows from abilities and gunfire.

segments.caption: The video segment follows Phoenix, a Duelist with fiery orange hands and a white jacket bearing an orange flame emblem, as he engages in intense combat across multiple sites in a fast-paced Valorant match. He wields a Vandal rifle with a dynamic skin that shifts between a blue and pink design and a gold and black design, showcasing precision fire during close-range and mid-range engagements. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently tracks enemy movements and site control, guiding Phoenix’s tactical navigation through areas like A Site, B Link, and C Garage. Early in the sequence, Phoenix secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies at A Site, highlighted by the 'ACE' notification and Japanese HUD text such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier eliminated). He then transitions to B Link, where he uses his 'Run It Back' ability—visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect—to recover from a near-death situation and eliminate an enemy. The environment shifts from futuristic indoor corridors to a traditional Japanese-style courtyard with wooden structures and paper lanterns, where Phoenix plants the Spike and engages in a firefight, again using 'Run It Back' to survive and secure kills. Japanese HUD text like 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable) appears, indicating a regional server setting. The sequence culminates in a chaotic firefight at A Main, where Phoenix, after being downed, activates 'Run It Back' to reverse time, heal, and eliminate multiple enemies, culminating in a 'WON' screen as the enemy team is eliminated. The Gelato Shop, with its colorful striped awning and ice cream display, serves as a visual landmark during outdoor combat. The entire sequence is punctuated by dynamic lighting, particle effects from abilities, and real-time combat feedback, emphasizing Phoenix’s aggressive playstyle and mastery of time-reversal mechanics.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with Phoenix, identifiable by his fiery orange hands and white jacket with an orange flame emblem, using his 'Run It Back' ability to reverse time after being hit, indicated by a glowing orange fist and a time-reversal effect. He is equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic skin that shifts from blue and pink to gold and black. The Mini-map in the top-left corner shows enemy movements and site locations, guiding Phoenix as he navigates through the map. He engages in combat near the Gelato Shop, a colorful storefront with a striped awning, and eliminates multiple enemies, securing an 'ACE' as confirmed by the on-screen notification. The Japanese HUD text appears, displaying messages like 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. Phoenix continues to use his 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, recovering from near-death situations, with the cooldown bar visible on the HUD. The environment shifts from outdoor areas with Mediterranean aesthetics to indoor corridors, where he plants the Spike and engages in close-range combat. The Blitz.gg Interface is briefly shown, displaying detailed statistics such as Combat Score and Headshot %, with a dark theme and green and purple accents. The Character Selection Screen appears, showing Phoenix as a Duelist with a description of his aggressive playstyle, and the Promotional Text Overlays promote Blitz.gg with phrases like 'and super easy to use', accompanied by a meme-style frog character with a thinking expression. The sequence ends with Phoenix successfully defusing the Spike at B Site, as indicated by the 'ALLY DEFUSING' notification, and the environment transitions to a more industrial setting with concrete walls and metal doors.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket and fiery orange hands during ability use, navigating the B Site area in a fast-paced Valorant match. He wields a Vandal rifle with a dynamic skin that shifts from blue and pink to gold and black, engaging enemies with precision. The Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks enemy movements and site control, guiding Phoenix’s tactical positioning. As he moves through B Main, he eliminates an enemy, triggering a 'SPIKE CARRIER KILLED' notification, and continues to push forward. The environment shifts from indoor corridors to the open Mid Market area, where he secures another kill, with the HUD displaying Japanese text such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a regional server setting. Phoenix activates his 'Run It Back' ability, shown by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, recovering from a near-death situation and securing an 'ACE' by eliminating all remaining enemies. The scene transitions to A Main, where he plants the Spike at A Rubble, and continues to eliminate enemies with his Vandal, culminating in a final kill that triggers a 'WON' screen. Throughout, the Blitz.gg interface overlays performance stats, and promotional text overlays appear with a meme-style frog character, adding a playful tone to the sponsorship message. The environment features Mediterranean-style architecture with warm lighting, transitioning to lush greenery in the A Site area, enhancing the visual contrast between zones.

active_entities.Japanese HUD Text: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. Appears in later frames, replacing English text and providing real-time game status updates.
active_entities.Blitz.gg Interface: A performance-tracking overlay and post-match analysis screen displaying detailed statistics such as Combat Score, Headshot %, Damage Per Round, and team coordination metrics. Features a dark theme with green and purple accents, and includes promotional text for Blitz.gg's analytics across multiple games.
active_entities.Promotional Text Overlays: On-screen text in bold white font with black outlines, promoting Blitz.gg with phrases like 'and super easy to use', accompanied by a meme-style frog character with a thinking expression, adding a playful tone to the sponsorship message.

discovered_new_entities.A Site Environment: A lush, green outdoor area with overgrown vegetation, stone pathways, and ancient-style architecture, contrasting with the earlier Mediterranean-style B Site. Features vibrant lighting and natural elements like moss-covered walls and water features, enhancing the visual atmosphere during combat.

segments.caption: The video segment follows Phoenix, a Duelist with fiery orange hands and a white jacket bearing an orange flame emblem, as he engages in intense combat across multiple sites in a vibrant, Mediterranean-inspired map. He wields a Vandal rifle with a dynamic skin that shifts between blue and pink, and later gold and black, showcasing high precision in eliminating enemies. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently tracks site locations (B Site, C Link, C Window, A Site) and enemy movements, guiding Phoenix’s tactical navigation. Early in the sequence, Phoenix uses his 'Run It Back' ability—visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect—to recover from a near-death situation, securing an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies. The environment transitions from the colorful Gelato Shop area with its striped awning to industrial zones with shipping containers and then to the lush, green A Site with stone pathways and overgrown vegetation. Japanese HUD text appears, displaying messages like 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a regional server setting. Phoenix’s combat is punctuated by close-range engagements, including a dramatic melee finish after planting the Spike, and he frequently activates his signature ability, with its cooldown bar visible on the HUD. The sequence culminates in a decisive victory, with Phoenix eliminating the final enemy and the screen flashing 'WON' as the enemy team is eliminated. The lighting shifts dynamically, from bright daylight in outdoor areas to more shadowed, enclosed spaces, enhancing the atmosphere of each combat zone.

active_entities.Phoenix: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Uses 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, and secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies.

segments.caption: The video segment follows Phoenix, a Duelist with fiery orange hands and a white jacket bearing an orange flame emblem, as he navigates a fast-paced Valorant match across multiple sites. Initially, he is seen in a narrow corridor near C Site, wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink skin, engaging enemies with precise gunfire. He activates his 'Run It Back' ability, shown by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a near-death situation and secure an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies in the area. The environment shifts from indoor corridors with wooden walls and orange accents to an outdoor Mediterranean-style area featuring the Gelato Shop with its striped awning and ice cream display. As the match progresses, Phoenix moves to B Main, where he continues to use his Vandal, now with a gold and black skin, to eliminate opponents. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently tracks site locations and enemy movements, aiding his tactical navigation. Japanese HUD text appears, indicating 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), suggesting a regional server setting. The lighting transitions from warm indoor tones to bright outdoor sunlight, enhancing the visual contrast between sites. Phoenix’s aggressive playstyle is highlighted as he plants the Spike and engages in close-range combat, with the Vandal’s skin shifting dynamically during combat. The sequence culminates in a high-intensity exchange near B Main, where Phoenix’s ability usage and precise shooting secure key eliminations, demonstrating his role as a dominant force in the match.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with Phoenix, identifiable by his fiery orange hands and white jacket with an orange flame emblem, navigating the B Site area of a vibrant Mediterranean-style map. He wields a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink skin, moving cautiously near a Gelato shop with a striped awning. The Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks enemy movements as Phoenix engages in combat, securing a kill and triggering a 'SPIKE PLANTED' notification. He then activates his 'Run It Back' ability, shown by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a near-death situation and eliminate multiple enemies, culminating in an 'ACE' notification. The scene transitions to a Japanese server setting, indicated by Japanese HUD text such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), as Phoenix moves through a modern industrial corridor with pink walls and metal structures. He switches to a gold and black Vandal skin, engaging enemies in close-quarters combat with precise shots. The environment shifts again to the lush, green A Site, featuring stone pathways and overgrown vegetation, where Phoenix continues to dominate with his 'Run It Back' ability, securing another 'ACE' after eliminating all enemies. The segment ends with a black screen displaying promotional text for Spotify and Twitter, indicating the player's social media handles.

segments.caption: The video segment transitions to a black screen with centered, static text overlays promoting social media handles. The text displays 'Spotify: Rawzu' in bright green and 'twitter.com/rawzuu' in light blue, both in a clean, sans-serif font with a subtle glow effect. The screen remains unchanged throughout the duration, serving as an end-card or outro sequence. There is no gameplay, character action, or environmental context present—only the promotional text against a solid black background, indicating the conclusion of the gameplay footage and a direct call to follow the player on social platforms.
